title: BI Is Dead, Long Live BI
optimized_headline: Is Business Intelligence Really Dead? Exploring Its Unexpected Evolution
raw_excerpt:
The true bottleneck was never the analysis.
The post BI Is Dead, Long Live BI appeared first on Towards Data Science.
raw_body:
The true bottleneck was never the analysis.
The post BI Is Dead, Long Live BI appeared first on Towards Data Science.
speedrun: The article argues that traditional Business Intelligence (BI) is outdated due to its reliance on static reports and delayed insights. Instead, it emphasizes the need for real-time data analytics and more interactive tools. This shift is crucial as businesses increasingly require immediate insights to stay competitive in a fast-paced market.
why_it_matters: Companies seeking timely decisions will benefit from adopting real-time analytics, improving their responsiveness to market changes.
This change signals a broader trend towards data-driven decision-making, potentially reshaping how industries approach information and strategy.

lenses.eli12: Think of traditional BI like a newspaper—great for information, but often outdated by the time you read it. In contrast, real-time analytics is like live news updates, keeping you informed as events unfold. This matters because it helps businesses adapt quickly to changes, making them more agile and competitive.
lenses.pm: For product managers, this shift means prioritizing tools that offer real-time insights over static reporting. Users increasingly expect immediate data access, which could enhance decision-making and operational efficiency. Focusing on these needs may lead to more successful products that align with market demands.
lenses.engineer: From a technical standpoint, the article highlights the limitations of traditional BI tools, which often rely on batch processing and static dashboards. In contrast, modern analytics platforms leverage real-time data streams and interactive visualizations, improving response times. Engineers should consider these advancements when developing data solutions to meet evolving business requirements.
